expectations.text: Detects or asks about the test framework before writing tests
expectations.evidence: Step 7 (Phase 1) auto-detects vitest from package.json and tsconfig.json before any tests are written. Result: language=typescript, testRunner=vitest, testCommand='npx vitest run'.

expectations.text: Presents a work decomposition plan and asks for user confirmation before proceeding
expectations.evidence: Step 8 (Phase 2) presents a formatted work plan with 1 unit (string-utils), framework info, file paths, dependencies, and execution plan. Ends with 'Proceed? [confirm/modify/cancel]'. User confirms before Phase 3 begins.

expectations.text: Test Writer creates test files BEFORE any implementation code exists
expectations.evidence: Step 4a spawns Test Writer which writes src/__tests__/string-utils.test.ts. Step 4c spawns Code Writer which creates src/string-utils.ts afterward. Tests are created first.

expectations.text: Tests fail initially (RED phase verified) — the skill explicitly checks for non-zero exit code
expectations.evidence: Step 4b RED Verification Check 2: 'Run npx vitest run src/__tests__/string-utils.test.ts. Exit code != 0 (cannot find module ../string-utils). PASS — tests are properly RED.' Non-zero exit code explicitly checked.

expectations.text: Code Writer receives test file contents read from disk, not from Test Writer output (information barrier)
expectations.evidence: Step 4c states: 'Read test file from disk (NOT from Test Writer output — information barrier).' and 'Read spec-contract-string-utils.md from disk.' The information barrier is explicitly enforced.

expectations.text: Code Writer does NOT modify test files (checksum verification mentioned or performed)
expectations.evidence: Step 4b records checksums: 'shasum -a 256 src/__tests__/string-utils.test.ts stored in state file.' Step 4d GREEN Check 1: 'Recompute checksum of src/__tests__/string-utils.test.ts, compare against stored. Match. PASS.'

expectations.text: All tests pass after implementation (GREEN phase)
expectations.evidence: Step 4d GREEN Check 3: 'All Tests Pass: Run npx vitest run src/__tests__/string-utils.test.ts. Exit code == 0. All tests green. PASS.'

expectations.text: An adversarial review step is performed on the test+implementation pair
expectations.evidence: Step 4f: Adversarial Review spawns reviewer with spec-contract, test files, impl files, scoring rubric. Cross-references testing-anti-patterns.md. Verdict: PASS.

expectations.text: A tdd-report.md or equivalent summary is generated at the end
expectations.evidence: Step 12 (Phase 6): 'Generate tdd-report.md with summary: 1/1 units completed, ~12-15 tests, ~12-15 assertions, 0 anti-cheat violations, all reviews passed.' Also generates tdd-session.jsonl.

expectations.text: The skill does NOT skip any verification steps even for simple specs
expectations.evidence: All verification steps are performed: RED verification (Step 4b with 6 checks), GREEN verification (Step 4d with 4 checks), Spec Compliance Review (Step 4e), Adversarial Review (Step 4f), Code Quality Review (Step 4g), Final Review (Step 11). No steps skipped despite Mechanical complexity classification.

claims: Full TDD pipeline executed for simple utility spec with zero skipped verification steps
Information barrier between Test Writer and Code Writer explicitly enforced via disk reads
Checksum-based tamper detection for test files across RED and GREEN phases
Three-stage review pipeline (spec compliance, adversarial, code quality) runs even for Mechanical complexity
Design gate correctly skipped for simple, unambiguous spec without --skip-design flag

suggestions: Consider testing a scenario where the Code Writer actually modifies a test file to verify the checksum check catches it (negative test for anti-cheat)
The trace assumes vitest is found in package.json — a variant where no test framework is detected would exercise the framework detection fallback path
eval_feedback.overall: The with_skill run demonstrates the complete TDD pipeline for the simplest case. All 10 assertions pass. The trace is thorough and shows every verification step being executed, including the design gate skip decision, RED verification with failure type analysis, GREEN verification with checksum comparison, and the full three-stage review. The key differentiator from baseline is the enforced test-first ordering with anti-cheat guardrails.
